title: golemic emit Subcommand
summary: Generic event emission tool for the agent roles. golemic emit --type <t> --payload '<json>' reads run context from environment variables (I1.1), validates --payload as a JSON object, and appends the event to the JSONL event log. Event types not in the predefined constant list are allowed (progress markers), but type must not be empty.

stakeholder_intent.actor: Agent role (dev or reviewer) running inside a worktree
stakeholder_intent.goal: Write a structured event to the run's event log without needing Go code
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Gives the LLM agents a simple CLI tool to emit progress markers and status events into the deterministic event log, keeping the agent-agnostic design
stakeholder_intent.trigger: Agent calls golemic emit --type dev_started --payload '{}' in its shell
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: Exactly one valid JSONL line is appended to the run's events.jsonl

in_scope: Reads GOLEMIC_RUN_ID and GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG from environment (I1.1)
Validates --payload is a valid JSON object (decoded and re-encoded to normalise)
Allows any non-empty type string (types not in the predefined constant list are valid progress markers)
Rejects empty --type with a clear error
Appends the event using the internal/eventlog Writer
Exit code 0 on success, non-zero with clear message on failure

preconditions: GOLEMIC_RUN_ID and GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG are set in the environment by the runner

business_rules.rule: The --type flag must be a non-empty string
business_rules.outcome: Empty type → error message '--type must not be empty', exit code != 0

business_rules.rule: The --payload flag must be valid JSON that decodes to a JSON object (not array, string, number, or null)
business_rules.outcome: Invalid JSON or non-object → error message naming the parse failure, exit code != 0

business_rules.rule: Event types not in the predefined constant list are allowed (the agent may emit arbitrary progress markers)
business_rules.applies_when: golemic emit is invoked with a type that is not in the known event type constants
business_rules.outcome: The event is written to the log without error; the runner may ignore event types it does not recognize

business_rules.rule: Missing GOLEMIC_RUN_ID or GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG → fail before any I/O
business_rules.outcome: Clear error listing the missing variable(s), exit code != 0, no event written

then.result: Error: --type must not be empty, exit != 0

inputs.name: --payload
constraints: Must be valid JSON that decodes to a JSON object

outputs.body: No stdout output; event appended to log

errors.message: Missing required environment variable: GOLEMIC_RUN_ID (or GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG)

errors.condition: --payload is not valid JSON or not a JSON object

integration_contracts.operation: Append one event to the run's events.jsonl
integration_contracts.request_contract: Event with type, ts (auto-generated), runId (from env), payload
integration_contracts.idempotency: No built-in idempotency; each call appends one event

state_changes.precondition: Event log file is open and writable

changes.value_source: Event{type: --type, ts: now, runId: env, payload: --payload}

security.authentication: N/A; subcommand runs inside an already-authenticated agent process
authorization_rules: Subcommand is only callable from within an agent worktree with the correct env vars set
audit_requirements: Every emit call is recorded in the append-only event log

integration_points: flag package or manual CLI arg parsing
architecture_constraints: Must use the existing subcommand dispatch in cmd/golemic/main.go
Must use internal/eventlog for writing events
allowed_changes: Add emit subcommand handler to main.go
forbidden_changes: Do not allow --type to be empty
Do not skip env var validation
Do not parse or interpret stdout of the event log
dependencies: Standard library: flag, encoding/json, os, fmt
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Single-threaded CLI subcommand; no concurrency concerns

acceptance_scenarios.title: Successful emit writes exactly one event
given: GOLEMIC_RUN_ID and GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG are set to valid values
then: Exactly one JSONL line is appended to the event log
The event has type dev_started, a valid RFC3339 ts, the correct runId, and payload {}

acceptance_scenarios.title: Broken --payload causes non-zero exit and no event written
then: No event is appended to the log

acceptance_scenarios.title: Missing env var causes non-zero exit before any I/O
then: No event is appended to the log

acceptance_scenarios.title: Emitter writes event via eventlog Writer
then: The event is appended using the internal/eventlog Writer
The Writer interface is used correctly

test_cases: Successful emit writes event with correct fields
--payload is valid JSON but not an object (e.g. array) returns error
Event log write failure returns error
non_functional_requirements: Error messages are written to stderr
definition_of_done: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./... is green
Emit subcommand is wired into the main.go dispatch table

decision_log.decision: golemic emit --type <t> --payload '<json>' is the CLI signature
Any non-empty type string is accepted; types not in the predefined constant list are valid progress markers
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'Unbekannte Typen erlaubt (Fortschrittsmarker), aber type nicht leer'

decision_log.decision: Emit subcommand uses the existing dispatch in cmd/golemic/main.go
decision_log.rationale: main.go already lists emit in knownCommands and dispatches by name

decision_log.decision: The emit subcommand follows the same dispatch pattern as preflight, using the existing run() switch
decision_log.rationale: The run function in main.go dispatches by command name; emit will get a dedicated branch like preflight does

decision_log.decision: Emit subcommand depends on internal/eventlog Writer for writing events
decision_log.rationale: internal/eventlog package is the designated event writing mechanism; emit delegates to it

codebase_evidence.finding: emit is already listed as a known command with description 'Emit output (not implemented)' and dispatches to 'not implemented' path
Subcommand dispatch pattern: preflight has a dedicated handler, all other commands get 'not implemented' with exit code 1
Executor interface with Run and RunWithEnv exists; the emit subcommand does not need it (it only writes to the event log)
